channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Family of DC plane crash victim files $250M claims against FAA, Army
item.description: The claims were filed on behalf of the widow and children of Casey Crafton, 40, of Salem. He died as a result of the crash, leaving behind his wife of 16 years, Rachel, and three young sons.
item.dc:creator: Brittany Schaefer, KRON4

item.title: Winter's next wave of storms takes aim at the East Coast
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — The latest in a long line of winter storms took direct aim at the East Coast on Wednesday, threatening to dump heavy snow and some ice across several states.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Latest: Tensions rise between Trump and Zelenskyy over the Russia-Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump on Wednesday called Volodymyr Zelenskyy a “dictator without elections,” lashing out after the Ukrainian president said Trump was being influenced by Russian disinformation as he moves to end the Kremlin’s war against Ukraine on terms that Kyiv says are too favorable to Moscow.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Suspects charged in connection to burglaries of several pro athletes
item.description: Seven Chilean nationals face federal charges related to the burglaries of several high profile athletes' homes.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dulle, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: North Carolina high school football players killed in ATV crash after house party; 3 adults charged
item.description: Three North Carolina adults have been arrested and charged in connection with a fatal August ATV crash that left two Rosman High School students dead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Americans' confidence in air travel safety dips slightly after Washington plane crash: AP-NORC poll
item.description: A new poll finds Americans’ confidence in air transportation and the federal agencies tasked with maintaining air safety has slipped a little from last year, following a recent crash in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Linley Sanders, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump signals 25 percent tariffs coming on cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday indicated he would unveil tariffs on imports of automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals in early April, starting at 25 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: All aboard Delta flight survive after plane flips while landing in Toronto
item.description: Communications between the tower and pilot were normal on approach and it's not clear what went so drastically wrong when the plane touched down.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Get a text about a $1,400 IRS stimulus check? Don't click the link, BBB says
item.description: If you get a text message claiming that you will receive a $1,400 check from the IRS, don’t click any links. The Better Business Bureau warns that it’s a scam.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The pope has pneumonia in both lungs but remains in good spirits, Vatican says
item.description: Francis was admitted to Rome’s Gemelli hospital in a “fair” condition on Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A look at recent aviation disasters in North America
item.description: The crash of a Delta Air Lines plane in Toronto is the fourth major aviation disaster in North America in the last month. Here's a look at each of the crashes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: WSJ asks Kennedy to cast aside 'antivax hooey' amid Texas measles outbreak
item.description: The Wall Street Journal's editorial board published on op-ed on Monday calling on Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. to set aside his anti-vaccine activist positions in light of the nearly 50 measles cases that have been confirmed among mostly unvaccinated children in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KRON4

item.title: More adults are wondering if they have autism: Tips to seek a diagnosis
item.description: More adults have sought insight on their own neurodiversity in the last decade — often after their children are diagnosed or after seeing social media posts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Police arrest apparent leader of cultlike 'Zizian' group linked to multiple killings in the US
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — The apparent leader of a cultlike group known as the Zizians has been arrested in Maryland along with another member of the group, Maryland State Police said Monday. Jack Lasota, 34, was arrested Sunday along with Michelle Zajko, 33, of Media, Pennsylvania.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: SpaceX employees visiting FAA amid reported firings
item.description: Members of the SpaceX team will "envision how we can make a new, better, modern and safer system," Sean Duffy said.
item.dc:creator: Steven Joachim, Fox 5

item.title: Trump administration cuts hit FDA employees in food safety, medical devices and tobacco products
item.description: Nearly half of the FDA’s $6.9 billion budget comes from fees paid by companies the agency regulates, including drug and medical device makers, which allows the agency to hire extra scientists to swiftly review products. Eliminating those positions will not reduce government spending.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: College student Jaren Barajas beats Damian Lillard in 3-point shooting contest, wins $100K
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Jaren Barajas shoots on his backyard hoop maybe once a month these days. He's a busy 18-year-old college student now, so basketball has taken a backburner to academics.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Stephen Curry captures MVP honors in All-Star Game at home, leads Shaq’s OGs to victory
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The final pregame introduction belonged to none other than Stephen Curry. The big MVP trophy at the end, Curry heaved that up into the air, too, in a fitting finish to his All-Star Game at home. His arena. His fans.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: William Byron avoids late wrecks to win 2nd straight Daytona 500
item.description: Ninth in the No. 24 Chevrolet with one lap left, Byron was in the right place by racing near the outside wall in overtime to became the first back-to-back winner since Denny Hamlin in 2019-20.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Shaq's OGs win the 1st NBA All-Star mini-tournament, topping Chuck's Global Stars 41-25 in the final
item.description: An All-Star Game like none other — since it was three games, not one — still had plenty of flash and not a lot of fire. And in the end, Boston's Jayson Tatum had the dunk that decided a crown.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: At least 9 people are dead, including 8 in Kentucky, in latest blast of winter weather
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — At least nine people have died in the most recent round of harsh weather to pummel the U.S., including eight people in Kentucky who died as creeks swelled from heavy rain and water covered roads. Kentucky Gov.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mother arrested after leaving 3 children to live alone for years
item.description: Oakland County Sheriff’s Office deputies arrested a 34-year-old Pontiac mother on Friday after she abandoned her three children for years, leaving them in horrendous living conditions.
item.dc:creator: Trevor King, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 3 dead in fiery Wyoming tunnel crash
item.description: Another person has been confirmed dead as the investigation continues into a major, multi-vehicle crash in the westbound tunnel of I-80 in Green River, Wyoming, on Friday, according to the Wyoming Dept. of Transportation.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, WGN 9

item.title: South Korean actress Kim Sae-ron found dead: reports
item.description: Kim Sae-ron, who appeared in Netflix's "Bloodhounds," among other projects, was found dead at her home on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: McDonald's teases 'new' chicken item, return of Snack Wraps in 2025
item.description: We knew Snack Wraps were returning to McDonald's this year, but now it looks like another item we've seen before is coming back.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mother of man tortured and killed in New York speaks out
item.description: “They are scum, they are evil, I don't know how somebody can be that evil," Linda Nordquist said of the people accused in the death of her son, Sam.
item.dc:creator: Adam Chodak, Fox 5

item.title: Seven more massage therapists accuse Ravens' Tucker of sexually inappropriate conduct: report
item.description: A total of 16 massage therapists from eight Baltimore-area spas have now shared accounts with the Baltimore Banner.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Democratic governors diverge on how to handle Trump
item.description: Democratic governors are diverging in their approaches to President Trump amid mounting speculation that some of them are gunning for the White House in 2028. Some are digging their heels into resistance efforts, as in Illinois, where Gov.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says US presented doc to access minerals, offered almost nothing in return
item.description: “I didn’t let the ministers sign a relevant agreement because in my view it is not ready to protect us, our interest,” Zelenskyy told The Associated Press on Saturday in Munich.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dismantling of federal efforts to monitor election interference creates opening for foreign meddling
item.description: “Our adversaries are upping their game every day,” said former Department of Homeland Security cyber chief Suzanne Spaulding. “I’m worried that we are, at the same time, tearing down our defenses.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Scientists are racing to discover the depth of ocean damage sparked by the LA wildfires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — On a recent Sunday, Tracy Quinn drove down the Pacific Coast Highway to assess damage wrought upon the coastline by the Palisades Fire. The water line was darkened by ash. Burnt remnants of washing machines and dryers and metal appliances were strewn about the shoreline.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Missouri community supports local Mexican restaurant after employees detained
item.description: One week after federal agents made an unannounced visit to El Potro Mexican Café in Liberty, their community came to help the restaurant on Valentine’s Day.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Sloan, ABC 4

item.title: Here are the agencies purging federal workers
item.description: The moves come as President Trump's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), led by tech billionaire Elon Musk, have exacerbated their efforts to cut down on government spending — in some cases looking to dismantle whole agencies or departments.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Investigators enter a highway tunnel in Wyoming where a fiery crash killed 2
item.description: GREEN RIVER, Wyo. (AP) — Investigators in Wyoming began sifting through the wreckage inside a highway tunnel Saturday after smoke, hazardous chemicals and structural concerns prevented them from determining how many people may have died in the fiery pileup.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sheryl Crow sells Tesla to donate funds to embattled NPR
item.description: “Money donated to NPR, which is under threat by President Musk, in hopes that the truth will continue to find its way to those willing to know the truth,” the country artist wrote.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, Fox 5

item.title: Democrats nervously wait on Trump response to court rulings
item.description: House Democrats howling over the flood of unilateral actions streaming from the White House are voicing confidence that the courts will provide a check on potential abuses of power. They’re less certain, however, about how President Trump will respond.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WGN 9
